pi_items.source: task.json and setup_task.sh downloading from rcsb.org
Downloaded PDB 5P21 and inspected HETATM records
verifier.py uses this as the minimum file size check
verifier.py enforces this lower bound; physically, G12 CA to GNP PG is ~4.34 Angstroms
verifier.py enforces this upper bound; physically, G12 CA to GNP PG is ~4.34 Angstroms
verifier.py requires >= 5 residues; python analysis found 17 distinct protein residues within 3.5 Angstroms of GNP
verifier.py requires >= 2 known key binding residues in the report

privileged_info_summary: The task operates on real structural data, using PDB ID 5P21, which represents human H-Ras p21 complexed with the non-hydrolyzable GTP analog GppNHp (ligand ID: GNP). Programmatic analysis of the downloaded PDB file confirms that the true physical distance between the C-alpha atom of Glycine 12 (G12 CA) and the gamma-phosphate of GNP (GNP PG) is approximately 4.34 Angstroms, falling safely within the verifier's allowed range of 3.0 to 8.0 Angstroms. Additionally, precisely 17 distinct protein residues are located within a 3.5 Angstrom radius of the GNP ligand atoms (including key residues 13, 16, 35, 60, 116, and 119), ensuring that the requirement to identify at least 5 binding residues and 2 key residues is functionally attainable in this structure.
